cards.title: 'Guaranteed 20% returns' offer
cards.script: Promises a fixed or guaranteed return on your money. Even SEBI-registered advisors are barred from doing this — anyone who does is illegal.
cards.example: Promising 20% returns on investment, deposit 5000 to start.

cards.title: Telegram VIP tips / signals
cards.script: Adds you to a Telegram or WhatsApp 'VIP signals' group claiming sure-shot stock or crypto calls. Pump-and-dump or fake-app withdrawal trap.
cards.example: Sure shot stock tip today, target 5%. Join VIP signals group on Telegram.

cards.title: Trading app pushed by stranger
cards.script: Stranger on WhatsApp / Telegram shows profit screenshots, gets you to deposit on an unknown app. Early withdrawals work; then withdrawals are blocked and they ask for 'tax' or 'GST' to release funds.
cards.example: Made ₹5000 in one day trading crypto, join my Telegram group.

cards.title: 'Double your money' / forex
cards.script: Promises to double your money in days or weeks via forex / crypto. No real investment exists.
cards.example: Double your money in 15 days! No risk forex trading. WhatsApp +91 9XXXXXXXXX.

red_flag_rules.red_flag: They are promising guaranteed / fixed / 'double your money' returns. SEBI bars Indian investment advisors from guaranteeing returns. This is a Ponzi or pump-and-dump pattern.
Telegram tip/signal groups offering 'sure-shot' calls are pump-and-dump or Ponzi setups. Anyone you can't verify on the SEBI Investment Adviser registry is operating illegally.

An unknown crypto / forex / trading app being pushed via WhatsApp or Telegram is the classic 'task-trading' scam — early withdrawals work, then you can't withdraw and they ask for tax/GST to release funds.
Asking for GST / income tax to 'release' your profits is a classic withdrawal-block scam. Real platforms deduct TDS from the payout, never ask you to pay first.

next_steps: Do not invest. Never deposit money to an app or person you found via Telegram, WhatsApp or unsolicited SMS.
Verify any 'investment advisor' on the SEBI registry: https://www.sebi.gov.in/sebiweb/other/OtherAction.do?doRecognisedFpi=yes
Report suspicious investment schemes on the RBI Sachet portal: https://sachet.rbi.org.in/
If you have already paid: call 1930 (cyber helpline) and file at https://cybercrime.gov.in. Speed matters — your bank can sometimes block the transfer in the first hours.
